storyBeat: 媽媽叮嚀去看奶奶 — 路上會發生什麼?

questions.speaker: grandma
questions.sentence: Tonight's story has a few new words. Let's learn them first!
questions.sentenceZh: 今晚的故事有幾個新字，我們先把它們學會！
questions.explanationZh: 奶奶說：今晚要講小紅帽的故事，先來認識幾個關鍵字，記住了故事更好聽喔！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match each Chinese word to its English.
questions.explanationZh:
小紅帽故事裡的關鍵字：
🔑 wolf = 狼
🔑 basket = 籃子
🔑 forest = 森林
🔑 hood = 帽子

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match each Chinese word to its English.
questions.explanationZh:
再來四個：
🔑 path = 路
🔑 flower = 花
🔑 grandma = 奶奶
🔑 village = 村子

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Which one is a wolf?
questions.question: Which one is a wolf?
questions.explanationZh: wolf = 狼 🐺！故事裡，森林裡藏著一隻大野狼，等著小紅帽出現。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Which one shows a forest?
questions.question: Which one shows a forest?
questions.explanationZh: forest = 森林 🌲！小紅帽走過森林去奶奶家，森林裡就住著那隻大野狼。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: She carried a basket of food to grandma's house.
questions.sentenceZh: 她提著一籃食物走去奶奶家。
questions.question: Which picture matches the sentence?
options: a girl reading a book at a desk
a girl running fast on a road
a girl sleeping in her warm bed
a girl walking with a basket on her arm
optionsZh: 一個女孩坐在桌前看書
一個女孩在暖床上睡覺
一個女孩手臂上挽著籃子走路
questions.explanationZh: basket = 籃子，句子說她「提著籃子走去奶奶家」——圖中正好是手挽著籃子在走路。

storyBeat: 森林裡見大野狼 — 他會做什麼?

questions.speaker: grandma
questions.sentence: A few more words to learn — then the story begins!
questions.sentenceZh: 再記幾個字，故事就要開始了！
questions.explanationZh: 奶奶說：再學幾個重要的字，學會了，故事就更好懂囉！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match each Chinese phrase to its English.
questions.explanationZh:
再來四個片語：
🔑 big teeth = 大牙齒
🔑 big eyes = 大眼睛
🔑 wooden door = 木門
🔑 feeling sick = 生病了

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Poor grandma was in bed. She was too sick to stand up.
questions.sentenceZh: 可憐的奶奶躺在床上，病得站不起來。
questions.question: Which picture matches 'grandma in bed, too sick to stand'?
options: an old woman cooking soup in her kitchen
an old woman lying in bed looking pale
an old woman sitting outside in the sun
an old woman dancing at a market
optionsZh: 一位老婆婆在廚房煮湯
一位老婆婆蒼白地躺在床上
一位老婆婆坐在外面曬太陽
一位老婆婆在市場跳舞
questions.explanationZh: bed = 床，sick = 生病。句子說奶奶躺在床上病得站不起來——圖中正好是蒼白的老婆婆躺在床上。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The wolf had long sharp teeth under his soft fur.
questions.question: Which emoji shows teeth?
questions.explanationZh: teeth = 牙齒 🦷！狼柔軟的毛皮下，藏著又長又尖的牙齒——小紅帽快看清楚！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The wolf ___ fast to grandma's house.
questions.sentenceEasy: The wolf ___ fast to grandma's house.
questions.sentenceHard: Without stopping even once, the wolf ___ fast through the trees to grandma's house.
questions.question: Which word is correct?
questions.explanationZh:
這是過去的故事——run 的過去式是 ran（不規則動詞）。
完整句：The wolf ran fast to grandma's house.

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Let's review all the new words from this lesson.
questions.explanationZh:
把這節的重點字再複習一遍：
🔑 bed = 床
🔑 sick = 生病
🔑 teeth = 牙齒
🔑 door = 門

storyBeat: 大野狼問路 — 她應該說嗎?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
今晚的字，記好：
🔑 morning = 早上
🔑 tell = 告訴
🔑 house = 家
🔑 oak = 橡樹
其中「橡樹」等一下你會聽到——它是一個重要的路標。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Good morning, little girl," the wolf said in a soft voice.
questions.explanationZh: 「早安，小女孩，」狼用輕柔的聲音說。奶奶說，那個聲音，讓人放下了戒心。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Where are you going so early today?"
questions.explanationZh: 「妳這麼早，要去哪裡呀？」狼問。奶奶說，這個問題，最好不要回答。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The girl did not know that wolves were bad inside.
questions.question: Why did the girl trust the wolf?
options: she had no fear of him
questions.explanationZh: 她不知道狼心裡是壞的——不知道危險，當然就不害怕，所以是「她不怕他」。
questions.sentenceZh: 女孩不知道狼的心裡是壞的。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: She told him everything in her small voice.
questions.explanationZh: 她用小小的聲音，把所有事都告訴了狼。奶奶說，孩子啊，有些事，不能告訴陌生人。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "I am going to grandma's house, down the path."
questions.explanationZh: 「我要去奶奶家，沿著這條路走，」她說。奶奶說，她把全部都說了，一個字都沒留。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Her house is by the three big oaks. She is sick today."
questions.question: What did the girl tell the wolf?
questions.explanationZh: 三棵橡樹旁邊、奶奶生病——這些全都說了，狼知道了「奶奶住哪」。
questions.sentenceZh: 「她的家在三棵大橡樹旁邊。她今天生病了。」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The wolf nodded. "Pick some flowers for grandma," he said.
questions.explanationZh: 狼點點頭，說：「去採些花給奶奶吧。」奶奶說，這句話，不是好意，是陷阱。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The wolf wanted the girl to stop and stay in the woods longer.
questions.questionEn: Was the wolf trying to help the girl?
questions.explanationZh: 狼想讓她在樹林裡多停留——採花的時間越長，狼就能更早到奶奶家，這是陷阱。答 No。
questions.sentenceZh: 狼想讓女孩在樹林裡多待一段時間。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Should you tell a stranger where you are going?
questions.question: Should you tell strangers?
questions.explanationZh: 媽媽說過不要跟陌生人說話——所以「絕對不能說」自己要去哪裡！
questions.sentenceZh: 你應該告訴陌生人你要去哪裡嗎？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The girl ran off to pick flowers. The wolf ran the other way...
questions.explanationZh: 女孩跑去採花，狼往另一個方向跑去——奶奶說，知道接下來會發生什麼的你，一定很緊張。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match the key words about trust and danger.
questions.explanationZh:
今晚的關鍵字：
🔑 stranger = 陌生人
🔑 reveal = 說出
🔑 sweet words = 甜言
🔑 trap = 陷阱

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Good morning, little girl," the wolf said in a soft voice.
questions.sentenceZh: 「早安，小女孩，」狼用輕柔的聲音說。
questions.question: How did the wolf begin talking to the girl?
options: greeted her in a kind voice
asked about her mother right away
questions.explanationZh: 「用輕柔的聲音說早安」——他用「用親切的聲音打招呼」，讓女孩放鬆了戒心。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The girl did not know that wolves were bad inside.
questions.sentenceZh: 女孩不知道狼心裡是壞的。
questions.questionEn: Did the girl understand that the wolf was dangerous?
questions.explanationZh: 「她不知道狼是壞的」——她只看見一雙溫柔的眼，根本不曉得危險在哪裡。答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: She told him everything in her small voice.
questions.sentenceZh: 她用小小的聲音，把所有事都告訴了狼。
questions.question: What mistake did the girl make?
options: dropped her basket in the mud
told the wolf all her plans
optionsZh: 把自己的計劃全告訴了狼
questions.explanationZh: 「把所有事都告訴了狼」——她的錯誤是「把自己的計劃全告訴了狼」，讓他知道了一切。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Her house is by the three big oaks. She is sick today."
questions.sentenceZh: 「她的家在三棵大橡樹旁邊。她今天生病了。」
questions.question: What dangerous information did the girl share?
questions.explanationZh: 「三棵橡樹旁邊，奶奶生病了」——女孩說出了「奶奶的確切位置」，這讓狼知道去哪裡。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The wolf nodded. "Pick some flowers for grandma," he said.
questions.sentenceZh: 狼點點頭，說：「去採些花給奶奶吧。」
questions.questionEn: Was the wolf's flower advice meant to help grandma?
questions.explanationZh: 狼讓她採花，是要她在林子裡多繞一會兒——那些花，根本不是給奶奶的心意。答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The girl ran off to pick flowers. The wolf ran the other way.
questions.sentenceZh: 女孩跑去採花，狼往另一個方向跑去。
questions.question: What was the wolf doing while the girl picked flowers?
options: sang a song in the forest
questions.explanationZh: 狼往「另一個方向跑去」——他「趕快先去奶奶家」，要搶在女孩前面到達。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The wolf ___ his head and smiled at the girl.
questions.sentenceZh: 狼點點頭，對女孩微笑。
questions.question: Which word fits the blank?
questions.explanationZh: 這是過去發生的故事——nod 的過去式是 nodded，所以填 nodded。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Should you share where you are going with a stranger?
questions.sentenceZh: 你應該告訴陌生人你要去哪裡嗎？
questions.question: What is the right thing to do?
questions.explanationZh: 媽媽說過不要跟陌生人說話——「絕對不告訴陌生人」你要去哪裡，這是最安全的！

storyBeat: 大野狼跑去奶奶家 — 奶奶怎麼樣了?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
今晚的字，學起來：
🔑 plan = 計劃
🔑 flower = 花
🔑 run = 跑
🔑 door = 門
其中「計劃」——狼的壞計劃，等一下就要出現了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The wolf ran fast to grandma's house.
questions.explanationZh: 狼飛快地往奶奶家跑去，一秒都不耽誤。奶奶說，他跑起來，像一陣風。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His feet did not stop. The trees flew past.
questions.question: How did the wolf travel?
questions.explanationZh: 腳不停下來，樹從眼前飛過——這就是「用最快速度」跑。
questions.sentenceZh: 狼的腳一直跑，樹在他眼前飛快掠過。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Back in the woods, the girl picked many bright flowers.
questions.explanationZh: 女孩在樹林裡，一朵一朵採著漂亮的花，慢慢走著，什麼都不知道。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The wolf moved like the wind. The girl moved like a soft cloud.
questions.questionEn: Would the wolf get there first?
questions.explanationZh: 狼像風一樣快，女孩像雲一樣慢——當小紅帽還在採花，狼的爪子已經摸到奶奶家門把了。答 Yes。
questions.sentenceZh: 狼跑得像風，女孩走得像雲。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The wolf came to grandma's little house at last.
questions.explanationZh: 狼終於來到奶奶那間小房子前面。奶奶說，那個小房子，不知道裡面的奶奶是什麼感覺。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He knocked on the wooden door. Knock, knock, knock.
questions.explanationZh: 他用爪子敲了敲木門——咚，咚，咚。奶奶說，那個聲音，聽起來好像很普通。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Open up. It is me, Little Red Riding Hood."
questions.question: What did the wolf say at the door?
questions.explanationZh: 「開門，是我，小紅帽。」狼裝成女孩的聲音——這就是「裝成那個女孩」。
questions.sentenceZh: 「開門，是我，小紅帽，」狼說。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Poor grandma was in bed. She was too sick to stand up.
questions.explanationZh: 可憐的奶奶躺在床上，病得連站起來都辦不到。奶奶說這句話，輕輕嘆了口氣。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: How did grandma feel?
questions.question: How did grandma feel?
questions.explanationZh: 在床上、站不起來——這就是「生病無力」的樣子。
questions.sentenceZh: 奶奶的感覺是怎樣的？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Come in, dear," grandma called from her bed. The door opened...
questions.explanationZh: 「進來吧，孩子，」奶奶從床上喊。門慢慢打開了……奶奶說，接下來，會讓你很擔心。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match the action words for this part.
questions.explanationZh:
今晚的動作字：
🔑 sprint = 快跑
🔑 knock = 敲門
🔑 pretend = 假裝
🔑 open the door = 開門

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The wolf ran fast to grandma's house.
questions.sentenceZh: 狼飛快地往奶奶家跑去。
questions.question: Why did the wolf run so fast?
options: he was scared of the dark
questions.explanationZh: 狼在女孩採花時先跑去奶奶家——他「要在女孩前面到達」，這是他壞計劃的一部分。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The wolf moved like the wind. The girl moved like a soft cloud.
questions.sentenceZh: 狼跑得像風，女孩走得像雲。
questions.questionEn: Was the wolf faster than the girl?
questions.explanationZh: 「像風」比「像雲」快多了——女孩還在花叢裡，狼早就消失在林子深處了。答 Yes。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He knocked on the wooden door. Knock, knock, knock.
questions.sentenceZh: 他用爪子敲了敲木門——咚，咚，咚。
questions.question: What did the wolf do when he reached grandma's house?
options: called out in his own voice
questions.explanationZh: 「敲了敲木門——咚，咚，咚」——他「敲了前門」，假裝是小紅帽。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Open up. It is me, Little Red Riding Hood."
questions.sentenceZh: 「開門，是我，小紅帽，」狼說。
questions.question: What was the wolf doing at the door?
questions.explanationZh: 「是我，小紅帽」——狼假冒女孩的聲音，是在「假裝成那個女孩」。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Poor grandma was in bed. She was too sick to stand up.
questions.sentenceZh: 可憐的奶奶躺在床上，病得連站起來都辦不到。
questions.questionEn: Was grandma strong enough to defend herself?
questions.explanationZh: 「病得站不起來」——床上的奶奶連替自己開口都費力，哪有力氣防禦。答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Back in the woods, the girl picked many bright flowers.
questions.sentenceZh: 女孩在樹林裡，一朵一朵採著漂亮的花，慢慢走著。
questions.question: What was the girl doing while the wolf reached grandma?
questions.explanationZh: 「女孩在樹林裡採著漂亮的花」——她毫不知情，還在「在樹林裡採花」。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The wolf ___ on the door three times.
questions.sentenceZh: 狼敲了三下門。
questions.question: Which word fits the blank?
questions.explanationZh: 這是過去故事中的動作——knock 的過去式是 knocked，所以填 knocked。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Come in, dear," grandma called from her bed.
questions.sentenceZh: 「進來吧，孩子，」奶奶從床上喊。
questions.question: How was grandma feeling when she called out?
questions.explanationZh: 奶奶病著躺在床上，以為是小紅帽來了——她「生病而不知道危險」在門外等著。

questions.sentence: 狼跑得很快。
questions.explanationZh: ran 是 run 的過去式，故事發生在過去，所以要用 ran，不是 run！

questions.sentence: 他敲了木門。
accept: He knocked on the wooden door.
The wolf knocked on the door.
The wolf knocked on the wooden door.
questions.explanationZh: knock on the door = 敲門，這是固定說法！knocked 是 knock 的過去式，記住 -ed 就對了。

questions.sentence: 可憐的奶奶躺在床上，她病了。
accept: Poor grandma was lying in bed.
questions.explanationZh: poor 在這裡是「可憐的」，不是「窮的」！was in bed = 躺在床上，是很地道的英文說法。

questions.sentence: The wolf ran fast to grandma's house. Back in the woods, the girl picked many bright flowers.
questions.question: Why did the wolf want the girl to pick flowers?
options: He wanted grandma to have a gift.
He needed more time to reach the house first.
He thought flowers would cheer the girl up.
He was afraid the girl would follow him.
optionsZh: 他需要更多時間，好先到奶奶家。
他覺得花會讓女孩開心。
questions.explanationZh: 狼叫女孩採花，是為了讓她在樹林裡多花時間——這樣他就能先到奶奶家，完成他的壞計劃。

storyBeat: 小紅帽到奶奶家 — 床上是奶奶嗎?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
今晚的字，記好：
🔑 cap = 帽子
🔑 bed = 床
🔑 blanket = 毯子
🔑 nose = 鼻子
這幾個字，等一下你會聽到狼在裝扮成奶奶。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The wolf took grandma into his stomach in one big bite.
questions.explanationZh: 狼把奶奶整個吞進肚子裡。奶奶說，這一段，小朋友聽了可能會很緊張——放心，後面有救援。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He put on grandma's sleeping cap. He climbed into her bed.
questions.question: What did the wolf do next?
questions.explanationZh: 他戴上奶奶的睡帽，爬進床鋪——這就是在「裝扮成奶奶」。
questions.sentenceZh: 他戴上奶奶的睡帽，爬進她的床。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He pulled the blanket up to his black wolf nose.
questions.explanationZh: 他把毯子拉高高，蓋到那黑色的狼鼻子上。奶奶說，幸好他遮得差一點。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Only his eyes and one bit of nose showed above the blanket.
questions.questionEn: Was the wolf trying to hide his face?
questions.explanationZh: 只有眼睛和一點點鼻子露出來——毯子拉這麼高，就是不讓人看清那張狼臉。答 Yes。
questions.sentenceZh: 毯子上面，只露出他的眼睛和一點點鼻子。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Little Red Riding Hood came in with her basket of flowers.
questions.explanationZh: 小紅帽抱著一籃花走進來，輕輕說：「奶奶，我來了。」奶奶說，她還不知道。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: How did grandma's room feel?
questions.question: How did the room feel?
questions.explanationZh: 狼躺在床上裝奶奶，房間裡有什麼不對勁——「暗暗的、怪怪的」就是那個感覺。
questions.sentenceZh: 奶奶的房間，給人什麼感覺？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The room was dark. Something looked very wrong.
questions.explanationZh: 房間很暗，有什麼地方不對——奶奶說，就算說不出哪裡不對，那個感覺，不說謊。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The shape in the bed did not look like her dear grandma.
questions.question: What did the girl notice?
questions.explanationZh: 床上那個樣子，不像她認識的奶奶——所以她注意到「奶奶看起來不一樣」。
questions.sentenceZh: 床上那個輪廓，看起來不像她親愛的奶奶。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: She stepped close to the bed and looked again.
questions.explanationZh: 她走近床邊，再仔細看了一次……奶奶說，這個時候，你一定很替她捏一把冷汗。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Big ears. Big eyes. Big teeth. Was this really grandma...?
questions.explanationZh: 大耳朵。大眼睛。大牙齒……這真的是奶奶嗎？奶奶說，你猜猜看。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match the words about disguise.
questions.explanationZh:
今晚的關鍵字：
🔑 disguise = 裝扮
🔑 cover = 遮住
🔑 show = 露出
🔑 strange = 奇怪

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He put on grandma's sleeping cap. He climbed into her bed.
questions.sentenceZh: 他戴上奶奶的睡帽，爬進她的床。
questions.question: Why did the wolf put on grandma's cap and get into her bed?
options: hoping to get a good night's sleep
to fool her into thinking he was grandma
questions.explanationZh: 戴帽、爬進床——這是「要騙女孩以為他是奶奶」的偽裝計劃。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He pulled the blanket up to his black wolf nose.
questions.sentenceZh: 他把毯子拉高高，蓋到那黑色的狼鼻子上。
questions.questionEn: Was the wolf's disguise perfect with nothing showing?
questions.explanationZh: 「拉到黑色狼鼻子上」——那個黑鼻子，奶奶絕對沒有，偽裝破了一個洞。答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Only his eyes and one bit of nose showed above the blanket.
questions.sentenceZh: 毯子上面，只露出他的眼睛和一點點鼻子。
questions.question: Why did the wolf pull the blanket so high?
questions.explanationZh: 「只有眼睛和一點鼻子露出來」——他把毯子拉這麼高，就是「遮住他的狼臉」。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The room was dark. Something looked very wrong.
questions.sentenceZh: 房間很暗，有什麼地方不對。
questions.question: What feeling did the girl get in grandma's room?
questions.explanationZh: 「房間很暗，有什麼不對」——那種感覺是「陰暗又令人不安」，說不出哪裡不對。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The shape in the bed did not look like her dear grandma.
questions.sentenceZh: 床上那個輪廓，看起來不像她親愛的奶奶。
questions.questionEn: Did everything look normal to the girl at first?
questions.explanationZh: 「不像她認識的奶奶」——那個輪廓太大了，不是每天在院子等她的那個奶奶。答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: She stepped close to the bed and looked again.
questions.sentenceZh: 她走近床邊，再仔細看了一次。
questions.question: What did the girl do when she sensed something was wrong?
questions.explanationZh: 「走近床邊，再仔細看了一次」——她選擇「走近仔細看」，雖然有點擔心。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The wolf ___ on grandma's sleeping cap.
questions.sentenceZh: 狼戴上了奶奶的睡帽。
questions.question: Which word fits the blank?
questions.explanationZh: put 是不規則動詞——過去式也是 put，不是 putted，所以選第一個。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Big ears. Big eyes. Big teeth. Was this really grandma?
questions.sentenceZh: 大耳朵。大眼睛。大牙齒……這真的是奶奶嗎？
questions.question: What might the girl be feeling now?
questions.explanationZh: 大耳、大眼、大牙——這樣的奶奶很不對勁，女孩一定「開始覺得害怕」了。

storyBeat: 獵人來救 — 來得及嗎?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
最後的字，學起來：
🔑 ear = 耳朵
🔑 teeth = 牙齒
🔑 hear = 聽
🔑 sleep = 睡
這幾個字，今晚故事的最後一段會用到。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Grandma, what big ears you have!" the girl said.
questions.explanationZh: 「奶奶，妳的耳朵好大喔！」女孩說。奶奶說，她還不知道危險，所以才敢問。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "All the better to hear you with, my dear."
questions.explanationZh: 「這樣才能聽得到妳呀，孩子。」奶奶說，那個聲音，跟真正的奶奶不太一樣。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Grandma, what big teeth you have!" "All the better to eat you with!"
questions.question: What did the wolf finally say?
questions.explanationZh: 「奶奶，妳的牙齒好大！」「這樣才能把妳吃掉呀！」——狼露出了真面目，他說的是「要吃女孩」。
questions.sentenceZh: 「奶奶，妳的牙齒好大！」「這樣才能把妳吃掉呀！」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The wolf jumped up. He took the girl into his stomach too.
questions.explanationZh: 狼跳起來，把女孩也吞進去了。奶奶說，到這裡，故事緊到讓人喘不過氣。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Then he was full. He fell back into bed and slept.
questions.explanationZh: 狼吃飽了，倒回床上就睡著了。奶奶說，他完全沒有心虛，太可惡了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Soon the whole house shook with loud snoring sounds.
questions.explanationZh: 整間屋子都被打呼聲震動——狼吃飽了，倒頭就睡，窗玻璃都在微微顫。答 Yes。
questions.sentenceZh: 很快，整間屋子都被大聲的打呼震動著。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A kind huntsman walked by the little house.
questions.explanationZh: 一個善良的獵人恰好走過那間小屋旁邊。奶奶說，「恰好」——這兩個字，真的很重要。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He heard the snoring. He knew that was no grandma sound.
questions.question: How did the huntsman know something was wrong?
questions.explanationZh: 他聽到那打呼聲，知道那不是奶奶會發出的聲音——是「聽到怪聲」讓他知道不對勁的。
questions.sentenceZh: 他聽到那打呼聲，知道這不是奶奶應該發出的聲音。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Who came to help in time?
questions.question: Who came to help?
questions.explanationZh: 走過來幫忙的，是那個善良的獵人——「善良的獵人」！
questions.sentenceZh: 誰及時趕來幫忙了？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The huntsman opened the door. He held his hunting knife ready...
questions.explanationZh: 獵人打開門。他握緊獵刀準備好……

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match the rescue words from tonight.
questions.explanationZh:
今晚的救援字：
🔑 snore = 打呼
🔑 knife = 刀
🔑 walk by = 走過
🔑 rescue = 救

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Grandma, what big ears you have!" the girl said.
questions.sentenceZh: 「奶奶，妳的耳朵好大喔！」女孩說。
questions.question: What was the girl really noticing?
options: those ears were much too big for grandma
grandma had got some new earrings
grandma surely needed a hearing aid
the ears looked so cute and fluffy
optionsZh: 那耳朵大到不像是奶奶的
耳朵看起來好可愛毛絨
questions.explanationZh: 她說「耳朵好大」——其實是注意到「耳朵大到不像是奶奶的」，心裡開始懷疑。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "All the better to hear you with, my dear."
questions.sentenceZh: 「這樣才能聽得到妳呀，孩子。」
questions.questionEn: Did the wolf's answer calm the girl completely?
questions.explanationZh: 她接著又問了眼睛、又問了牙齒——心裡那個小小的警覺，一直沒有平靜下來。答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Grandma, what big teeth you have!" "All the better to eat you with!"
questions.sentenceZh: 「奶奶，妳的牙齒好大！」「這樣才能把妳吃掉呀！」
questions.question: What did the wolf's last answer finally reveal?
options: he just liked the girl's hat
his true plan to eat her
he wanted to leave at once
questions.explanationZh: 「這樣才能把妳吃掉」——狼說出了「他真正要吃她的計劃」，偽裝終於戳破！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The wolf jumped up. He took the girl into his stomach too.
questions.sentenceZh: 狼跳起來，把女孩也吞進去了。
questions.question: What happened to the girl?
questions.explanationZh: 「把女孩也吞進去了」——女孩「被狼吞進去了」，這是故事最緊張的一刻！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Soon the whole house shook with loud snoring sounds.
questions.sentenceZh: 很快，整間屋子都被大聲的打呼震動著。
questions.questionEn: Was the wolf awake and alert after eating?
questions.explanationZh: 整間屋子都在打呼震動——狼睡得像一塊大石頭，什麼都不知道了。答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A kind huntsman walked by the little house.
questions.sentenceZh: 一個善良的獵人恰好走過那間小屋旁邊。
questions.question: What was lucky about the huntsman walking by?
options: already searching for the wolf nearby
passing by at just the right time
questions.explanationZh: 「恰好走過」——奶奶說那兩個字很重要，他「恰好在正確時機走過」，救了兩條命。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He ___ the snoring and knew something was wrong.
questions.sentenceZh: 他聽到打呼聲，知道有什麼不對勁。
questions.question: Which word fits the blank?
questions.explanationZh: 這是過去發生的事——hear 的過去式是 heard，所以填 heard。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The huntsman opened the door. He held his hunting knife ready.
questions.sentenceZh: 獵人打開門，握緊獵刀準備好了。
questions.question: How did the huntsman prepare to enter?
questions.explanationZh: 「握緊獵刀準備好了」——他「拿著武器準備行動」，毫不猶豫地走進去。

storyBeat: 救出奶奶 — 媽媽的話現在懂了

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 huntsman = 獵人
🔑 open = 打開
🔑 pull = 拉
🔑 listen = 聽
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The huntsman saw the wolf fast asleep in grandma's bed.
questions.explanationZh: 獵人看見狼在奶奶床上熟睡。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He opened up the wolf with great care.
questions.question: What did the huntsman do to the wolf?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: opened up with care → 小心打開他 (paraphrase)。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He pulled grandma out first. Then he pulled the girl out too.
questions.explanationZh: 他先把奶奶拉出來。然後把女孩也拉出來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Grandma sat up. The girl held her hand. Both could breathe again.
questions.explanationZh: 奶奶坐起來了，小紅帽握著她的手，兩個人都在呼吸——她們都好好的。答 Yes。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The big bad wolf gave up. He never came back to those woods.
questions.explanationZh: 大野狼放棄了。他再也沒回到那片森林。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The huntsman walked home. Grandma drank some hot tea.
questions.question: How did the day end for grandma?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: hot tea + huntsman home → 溫暖又安全 (paraphrase)。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Little Red Riding Hood looked out the window.
questions.explanationZh: 小紅帽往窗外看。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "I will always stay on the path," she said.
questions.explanationZh: 「我以後一定走在路上,」她說。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: What did the girl learn from the day?
questions.question: What did she learn?
questions.explanationZh: 結尾教訓:聽媽媽的話。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "I will always listen to mother." And the red hood stayed bright forever...
questions.explanationZh: 「我會永遠聽媽媽的話。」紅帽子永遠鮮紅……

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match the resolution words from the ending.
questions.explanationZh:
今晚結尾的關鍵字：
🔑 pull out = 救出
🔑 safe = 安全
🔑 lesson = 教訓
🔑 forever = 永遠

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The huntsman saw the wolf fast asleep in grandma's bed.
questions.sentenceZh: 獵人看見狼在奶奶床上熟睡。
questions.question: What did the huntsman find when he entered?
options: the wolf sleeping in the bed
the girl hiding behind the door
questions.explanationZh: 「看見狼在奶奶床上熟睡」——他進門就看到「狼在床上睡著了」。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He pulled grandma out first. Then he pulled the girl out too.
questions.sentenceZh: 他先把奶奶拉出來。然後把女孩也拉出來。
questions.questionEn: Was the girl saved before grandma?
questions.explanationZh: 「先救出奶奶，再救出女孩」——獵人先把奶奶輕輕拉出來，再換小紅帽，順序不一樣。答 No。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Grandma sat up. The girl held her hand. Both could breathe again.
questions.sentenceZh: 奶奶坐起來，女孩握著她的手，兩個人都能再呼吸了。
questions.question: What tells us that both grandma and the girl were safe?
options: both sat up and breathed again
optionsZh: 兩個人都坐起來能呼吸了
questions.explanationZh: 「坐起來、能呼吸了」——「兩個人都坐起來能呼吸了」，這就是她們都安全的證明。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The big bad wolf gave up. He never came back to those woods.
questions.sentenceZh: 大野狼放棄了，他再也沒回到那片森林。
questions.question: What happened to the wolf at the end?
options: 😴 went back to sleep all day
questions.explanationZh: 「再也沒回到那片森林」——狼「離開後再也沒回來」了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "I will always stay on the path," she said.
questions.sentenceZh: 「我以後一定走在路上，」她說。
questions.questionEn: Did the girl learn from what happened?
questions.explanationZh: 「我以後一定走在路上」——她終於懂了媽媽那句話的重量，不只是規定，是愛。答 Yes。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "I will always listen to mother." And the red hood stayed bright forever.
questions.sentenceZh: 「我會永遠聽媽媽的話。」紅帽子永遠鮮紅……
questions.question: What was the main lesson of this story?
options: never go into a forest alone
questions.explanationZh: 「永遠聽媽媽的話」——這個故事最大的教訓就是「聽從父母的話」，他們的叮嚀是保護你的。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The huntsman ___ grandma out of the wolf safely.
questions.sentenceZh: 獵人安全地把奶奶從狼肚子裡拉出來。
questions.question: Which word fits the blank?
questions.explanationZh: 這是過去發生的救援動作——pull 的過去式是 pulled，所以填 pulled。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "I will always stay on the path," she said.
questions.sentenceZh: 「我以後一定走在路上，」她說。
questions.question: How did the girl feel as she said this?
questions.explanationZh: 她下定決心要聽媽媽的話——歷經危險後，她「更有智慧更謹慎了」。
